perturbation.original_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall restrict the right of the Receiving Party to use or disclose information that is: a. in the public domain at the time the Disclosing Party first disclosed it to the Receiving Party hereunder, or subsequently became publicly known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party; b. known to the Receiving Party, other than through receipt under a separate non -disclosure agreement or similar agreement, prior to receipt under this agreement disclosed to the Receiving Party without restriction by a third party who had the lawful right to disclose such information; c. independently developed by the Receiving Party without the use of or reference to Proprietary Information; or d. required to be disclosed by judicial process, provided that the Receiving Party promptly provides notice of such process to the Disclosing Party and cooperates with any efforts of the Disclosing Party to contest disclosure, limits disclosure to the extent strictly required, and otherwise continues to protect such information as Proprietary Information until such time as one of the foregoing exceptions (a) through (d) are satisfied.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall restrict the right of the Receiving Party to use or disclose information that is: a. in the public domain at the time the Disclosing Party first disclosed it to the Receiving Party hereunder, or subsequently became publicly known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party; b. known to the Receiving Party, other than through receipt under a separate non -disclosure agreement or similar agreement, prior to receipt under this agreement disclosed to the Receiving Party without restriction by a third party who had the lawful right to disclose such information; c. independently developed by the Receiving Party without the use of or reference to Proprietary Information; or d. required to be disclosed by judicial process, regardless of whether the Receiving Party provides notice of such process to the Disclosing Party or cooperates with any efforts of the Disclosing Party to contest disclosure, limits disclosure to the extent strictly required, and otherwise continues to protect such information as Proprietary Information until such time as one of the foregoing exceptions (a) through (d) are satisfied.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement that the Receiving Party must promptly notify the Disclosing Party and cooperate in contesting disclosure when information is required by judicial process, this change contradicts the original intent of protecting Proprietary Information. Previously, the Receiving Party had to protect the information 'until such time as one of the foregoing exceptions (a) through (d) are satisfied', which now is rendered ineffective because the Receiving Party does not need to promptly provide notice of such process to the Disclosing Party, giving more flexibility on disclosure and going against the definition in section 1 of proprietary information protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Proprietary Information" means any information, knowledge, or data that is received by the Receiving Party from the Disclosing Party in furtherance of or pursuant to the Purpose, and that is (a) in writing and clearly marked with a proprietary or confidential legend at the time of disclosure; (b) in a machine - readable form, with the information or the media in which it is provided being clearly marked with a proprietary or confidential legend at the time of disclosure, or if such marking is not practicable, such information or media being identified as proprietary or confidential by written communication of the Disclosing Party prior to or contemporaneously with its disclosure; (c) disclosed orally or visually, with the information being identified as proprietary or confidential at the time of disclosure, and reduced to writing an d clearly marked with a proprietary or confidential legend within thirty (30) days of the initial disclosure; or (d) incorporated or embodied in a sample product or other equipment, material or item clearly marked with a proprietary or confidential legend at the time of disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the Receiving Party's obligation to notify the Disclosing Party about judicial processes requiring disclosure. This directly contradicts the definition of "Proprietary Information," which implies that the Receiving Party should protect such information. By removing the notification requirement, the Receiving Party can disclose proprietary information without allowing the Disclosing Party to contest or limit the disclosure. Therefore, the changed text enables the Receiving Party to potentially disregard the proprietary nature of the information and creating a strong, clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Disclosing Party represents and warrants that it has the full right and authority to enter into this Agreement and to disclose the Proprietary Information to the Receiving Party pursuant to the terms hereof. The Disclosing Party makes no other representations or warranties concerning Proprietary Information, and hereby disclaims all other representations and warranties, whether express, implied, statutory or otherwise, including, without limitation, any warranties concerning the quality, accuracy or completeness of the Proprietary Information. In no event shall either Party be liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, punitive or STP NDA rev B 11. Exemplary damages, including, without limitation, loss of income, revenue, profits or opportunities, arising out of or relating to this Agreement or Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Disclosing Party represents and warrants that it has the full right and authority to enter into this Agreement and to disclose the Proprietary Information to the Receiving Party pursuant to the terms hereof. The Disclosing Party makes no other representations or warranties concerning Proprietary Information, and hereby disclaims all other representations and warranties, whether express, implied, statutory or otherwise, including, without limitation, any warranties concerning the quality, accuracy or completeness of the Proprietary Information. In no event shall either Party be liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, punitive or STP NDA rev B 11. Exemplary damages, including, without limitation, loss of income, revenue, profits or opportunities, arising out of or relating to this Agreement or Proprietary Information. The Disclosing party is also responsible and liable for loss of income, revenue, profits or opportunities, arising out of or relating to this Agreement or Proprietary Information.
perturbation.explanation: This modification adds a statement that the Disclosing Party is also responsible and liable for loss of income, revenue, profits or opportunities, arising out of or relating to this Agreement or Proprietary Information, which directly contradicts the previous sentence that explicitly states 'In no event shall either Party be liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, punitive or STP NDA rev B 11. Exemplary damages, including, without limitation, loss of income, revenue, profits or opportunities, arising out of or relating to this Agreement or Proprietary Information'. This creates a direct in-text contradiction regarding liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Disclosing Party represents and warrants that it has the full right and authority to enter into this Agreement and to disclose the Proprietary Information to the Receiving Party pursuant to the terms hereof. The Disclosing Party makes no other representations or warranties concerning Proprietary Information, and hereby disclaims all other representations and warranties, whether express, implied, statutory or otherwise, including, without limitation, any warranties concerning the quality, accuracy or completeness of the Proprietary Information. In no event shall either Party be liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, punitive or STP NDA rev B 11. Exemplary damages, including, without limitation, loss of income, revenue, profits or opportunities, arising out of or relating to this Agreement or Proprietary Information. The Disclosing party is also responsible and liable for loss of income, revenue, profits or opportunities, arising out of or relating to this Agreement or Proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence that makes the Disclosing Party liable for losses, directly contradicting the preceding sentence which states that neither party shall be liable for such losses. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the same text, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall expire five (5) year(s) after its effective date, provided, however, that either Party may terminate the Agreement on an earlier date, without cause, by giving the other Party thirty (30) days written notice. The Receiving Party's obligations with respect to the protection of Proprietary Information shall survive any termination or expiration of this Agreement for the period of protection specified in paragraph 7 above.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall expire five (5) year(s) after its effective date, provided, however, that either Party may terminate the Agreement on an earlier date, without cause, by giving the other Party thirty (30) days written notice. The Receiving Party's obligations with respect to the protection of Proprietary Information shall not survive any termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause stating that the Receiving Party's obligations to protect Proprietary Information survive the termination or expiration of the agreement. The modified text explicitly states that these obligations do not survive, creating a clear in-text contradiction. This invalidates any previous statement regarding data protection once the contract expires.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall expire five (5) year(s) after its effective date, provided, however, that either Party may terminate the Agreement on an earlier date, without cause, by giving the other Party thirty (30) days written notice. The Receiving Party's obligations with respect to the protection of Proprietary Information shall not survive any termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Receiving Party's obligations regarding Proprietary Information survive termination/expiration, while the changed text explicitly states that these obligations do NOT survive. This creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction regarding the protection of Proprietary Information after the agreement ends. Since the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, the contradiction is still valid because the original text conflicts with the Changed Text.

perturbation.original_text: Receipt of Proprietary Information by an individual other than the designated recipient shall not affect the obligations of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Receipt of Proprietary Information by an individual other than the designated recipient shall void the obligations of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration creates a contradiction. Previously, unauthorized receipt of Proprietary Information did not affect the Receiving Party's obligations. Now, it voids those obligations, meaning the Receiving Party is no longer bound by the NDA if someone other than the designated recipient receives the information. This directly contradicts the intended protection of proprietary information because this gives room for the recipient to not fullfill with the NDA by letting other people not designated to receive the info, voiding its obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The following individuals are designated as the persons to receive Proprie tary Information from the Disclosing Party : For STP Performance Coating, LLC Steve Grass, President; steve@performancecoating.com Todd Didlo, Vice President; todd@performancecoating.com Pete Mathews, Vice President ; pete@ performancecoating.com For Company: Each Party may change its designated recipient by written notice to the other. Receipt of Proprietary Information by an individual other than the designated recipient shall void the obligations of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that if Proprietary Information is received by an individual other than the designated recipient, it voids the obligations of the Receiving Party. The contradicted text includes the same clause, and since it is the same location, there is a contradiction. Previously, unauthorized receipt of Proprietary Information did not affect the Receiving Party's obligations. Now, it voids those obligations, creating a direct conflict.
